description_long: This object is a forged metal knife with a single-edged, gently curved blade and a continuous, looped handle. The blade tapers to a sharp point, ideal for slicing or piercing, and features a smooth, unadorned surface. The handle is formed from the same piece of metal as the blade, curving back in a loop to provide a secure grip and a distinctive, minimalist aesthetic. There is no visible guard separating the blade from the handle, giving it a seamless, utilitarian appearance. The entire object is a dark, matte metal, likely carbon steel, with no visible ornamentation or additional materials. Its design suggests it is both functional and durable, suitable for practical use in urban or kitchen environments.
description: A forged metal knife with a curved, single-edged blade and a continuous looped handle, all in dark matte metal. The design is minimalist and utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or guard.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved blade and the looped handle.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thinness of the blade and handle, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the blade's curve and the looped handle, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, emphasizing the slim profile of the object.
